properties.name: Into the Market
properties.description: The archway opens onto the south end of the market square. The noise and bustle grow louder ahead.

properties.name: The Gate
properties.description: The stone archway back to the market entrance.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: The square opens up ahead, thronging with people and stalls.

properties.name: South End
properties.description: The quieter south end of the square, near the gate.

properties.name: Rawstock Row
properties.description: A narrow lane lined with materials vendors branches off to the east.

properties.name: The Open Forge
properties.description: The glow of a forge and the sound of hammering draw you west, under a heavy canvas awning.

properties.name: Oddments Alley
properties.description: A narrow side passage angles off to the northeast, quieter than the main square.

properties.name: The Rendering Yards
properties.description: The cobblestones give way to iron plate. The noise gets louder and more industrial.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: The lane opens back out into the bustle of the market square.

properties.name: Tiv's Materials
properties.description: A cramped stall ahead, shelves crammed with bins and jars.

properties.destinationIntent: The back end of Rawstock Row — bulk materials, wholesale vendors, maybe a warehouse or loading dock where goods arrive from other dimensions. More industrial, less retail. Might hint at how materials actually get to the market.
properties.name: Loading End
properties.description: The lane continues east, the stalls giving way to larger bins and stacked crates.

properties.name: Rawstock Row
properties.description: Back out into the narrow lane of materials vendors.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: Back into the open market square.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: The passage opens back into the busy market square.

properties.name: The Fair Exchange
properties.description: The alley opens into a quieter court. The stalls here look more permanent.

properties.name: Oddments Alley
properties.description: Back through the narrow passage toward the main market.

properties.name: The Broker's Nook
properties.description: A small stall at the back, walled in ledgers.

properties.name: The Fair Exchange
properties.description: Back out into the open court.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: Back into the bustle of the market square. The cobblestones feel civilized after the iron plate.

properties.name: The Smelting Bay
properties.description: The heat intensifies ahead. Crucibles glow in a wide workspace.

properties.name: The Rendering Yards
properties.description: Back toward the entrance. The air cools slightly.

properties.name: The Nut Cart
properties.description: A brass cart tended by a vendor with too many elbows. Smells good.

properties.name: Market Square, South End
properties.description: Back into the south end of the square.

properties.name: Wire & Filament
properties.description: A stall draped in coils of wire, run by a vendor who doesn't look up.

properties.name: Rawstock Row
properties.description: Back out into the lane.

properties.name: Food Row
properties.description: Steam and the smell of cooking rise from stalls to the northwest.

properties.name: Market Square
properties.description: Back into the main square.

properties.name: The Lens Grinder
properties.description: A workbench under a canvas awning, covered in glass dust.

properties.name: Market Square, South End
properties.description: Back into the south end of the square.

properties.name: The Bindery
properties.description: A stall hung with sheets of pressed fiber. Thread glows at the needle.

properties.name: The Fair Exchange
properties.description: Back into the court.

properties.name: The Press House
properties.description: A shed-sized machine cranks and hisses to the east.

properties.name: The Rendering Yards
properties.description: Back toward the entrance.

properties.destinationIntent: A drink stall or tea house deeper in Food Row. Serves hot drinks from different dimensions — some glow, some steam in unusual colors. A quieter spot where vendors come to sit and talk. Maybe a vendor who trades in rumors along with beverages.
properties.name: The Tea Stall
properties.description: A quieter spot further along, where steam rises from cups that glow faintly.

properties.destinationIntent: A spice vendor's stall — bins of powders and dried things from dozens of dimensions. Some spices have mild material properties (a spice that makes things temporarily magnetic, one that changes color when it touches something from a specific dimension). The vendor organizes by effect, not by origin.
properties.name: The Spice Stall
properties.description: Bins of powders in colors you didn't know existed. The air prickles.

properties.destinationIntent: A stall selling sounds in jars — corked glass bottles that emit noises when opened. Some are useful (a jar of silence for sleeping in a noisy market), some are strange (the sound of a door closing in a dimension that doesn't have doors). The vendor collects sounds the way others collect materials.
properties.name: The Sound Vendor
properties.description: Glass jars line the shelves. One of them is humming.

properties.destinationIntent: A stall deeper in the Fair Exchange where the vendor trades in time — literally. They'll buy an hour of your future and sell you an hour of someone else's past. The transactions are precise and always fair. The vendor keeps time in small hourglasses filled with sand of different colors.
properties.name: The Time Merchant
properties.description: A stall where hourglasses line the shelves, each filled with differently colored sand.

properties.destinationIntent: A fiber workshop where raw dimensional materials are drawn into thread and rope. A vendor operates a spinning wheel that pulls material through a dimensional aperture to thin it. The resulting fibers have unusual properties depending on the source material. More craft-focused than the smelting bay, dealing in flexible materials rather than rigid ones.
properties.name: The Fiber Works
properties.description: A spinning wheel whirs. Thread of impossible thinness accumulates on spools.

properties.destinationIntent: A quenching yard — vats of different liquids for cooling hot materials. Some vats contain water, some contain oils, one contains a liquid that isn't quite there. The properties of the quenching medium affect the final material. An old worker tends the vats and knows which liquid to use for what.
properties.name: The Quench Yard
properties.description: Vats of liquid line the wall. Steam rises from one. Another is perfectly still.
